site_tagline: A FORTRAN 66/77 & DEC FORTRAN-10 interpreter, in Python.
site_description: Written in pure Python, with two pluggable axes: the front-end dialect — strict ANSI FORTRAN 66 by default, full ANSI FORTRAN 77, or the DEC FORTRAN-10 superset — and the machine value model — a 64-bit default, a faithful 36-bit PDP-10, or a little-endian LP64. Conformance is checked against the FCVS corpus; the PDP-10 model against the DEC FORTRAN-10 V5 manual and real 1970s code, the LP64 model against gfortran.
attribution: Software Architecture, Design & Engineering by Nicholas J. Kisseberth.
Code Synthesized via Anthropic Claude Code / Opus 4.8.
Automated Code Review via OpenAI Codex / ChatGPT 5.5.

docs.title: FORTRAN 77 reference
docs.summary: A complete, example-driven reference manual for the FORTRAN 77 language (ANSI X3.9-1978) — forterp's default dialect — organized on the standard: data types, arrays, expressions, control flow, I/O and FORMAT, procedures, and the intrinsic library, with a forterp notes box per chapter.

docs.title: FORTRAN 66 reference
docs.summary: A complete, example-driven reference manual for the FORTRAN 66 language (USA Standard X3.9-1966) plus the DEC FORTRAN-10 extensions, organized on the standard: program form, data types, expressions, statements, procedures, and the intrinsic library, with a forterp notes box per chapter. The strict-subset dialect (forterp.F66).

docs.title: The forterp manual
docs.summary: The guide to the interpreter itself: running it (CLI + the interactive processor), driving it from Python (run_source, the Interpreter class, custom host routines), the target/dialect axes and faithful type punning, and how it works inside (the pipeline, the memory model, the seams, testing). A chaptered manual.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.summary: Dated history of the standalone interpreter, in order: the target/dialect axes, the CLI and monitor, the conformance work, and the review sweep.

docs.title: Documentation
docs.summary: Index of the forterp documentation and the authoritative standards it targets.
